feed.title: the0cp
feed.subtitle: Jigsaw falling into place. So there is nothing to explain.

entry.title: Building a Register VM Interpreter - Part 10&#58 Heap, Strings, and Hash Tables

link.title: Building a Register VM Interpreter - Part 10&#58 Heap, Strings, and Hash Tables

entry.summary: This guide is about building a register-based VM interpreter from scratch. To escape this 64-bit limit, we have to look beyond the virtual stack and into the C heap.

entry.title: Building a Register VM Interpreter - Part 9&#58 The Control Flow

link.title: Building a Register VM Interpreter - Part 9&#58 The Control Flow

entry.summary: This guide is about building a register-based VM interpreter from scratch. We will upgrade the VM to understand Jump instructions, and more importantly, we will teach the single-pass compiler how to calculate exactly where those jumps should land, even when it hasn't finished compiling the code yet.

entry.title: Building a Register VM Interpreter - Part 8&#58 The VM Core

link.title: Building a Register VM Interpreter - Part 8&#58 The VM Core

entry.summary: This guide is about building a register-based VM interpreter from scratch. We will design the memory structures required to run a program, build the dispatch loop to fetch instructions, and write the logic to finally execute the math and logic operations we worked so hard to compile.

entry.title: Building a Register VM Interpreter - Part 7&#58 Expression Descriptor and Register

link.title: Building a Register VM Interpreter - Part 7&#58 Expression Descriptor and Register

entry.summary: This guide is about building a register-based VM interpreter from scratch. We need a mechanism to delay emitting bytecode until we know exactly where the data lives. We need a way to describe an expression's state while it is still in transit.

entry.title: Building a Register VM Interpreter - Part 6&#58 Single-Pass Pratt Parsing

link.title: Building a Register VM Interpreter - Part 6&#58 Single-Pass Pratt Parsing

entry.summary: This guide is about building a register-based VM interpreter from scratch. In a single-pass architecture, we do not build an AST. There are no intermediate tree nodes sitting in memory. Instead, we read a token, figure out what it means in its context, and emit the bytecode for it right then and there.
